description: M6 Dev Browser — Lightpanda integration, agent browser tools, developer visual editor, Inspect in agent

userStories.title: Create browser engine abstraction layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an abstraction over browser engines so we can swap between Lightpanda and Playwright.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/browser/types.ts with BrowserEngine interface: launch(), navigate(url), click(selector), fill(selector, value), screenshot(), getText(selector), evaluate(script), close()
Define BrowserConfig type: { engine: 'lightpanda' | 'playwright', headless: boolean }
Export types from server/browser/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement Playwright browser adapter
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Playwright-based browser adapter as the initial implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/browser/playwright-adapter.ts implementing BrowserEngine
launch() starts a Chromium browser instance via Playwright
All interface methods delegate to Playwright's Page API
screenshot() returns a base64 PNG string
evaluate(script) runs arbitrary JS in the page context and returns the result
close() shuts down the browser instance
Export from server/browser/index.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Lightpanda adapter will be added when Lightpanda reaches stability gate. Playwright is the initial default.

userStories.title: Create browser-test Ability with MCP tools
userStories.description: As a developer, I need MCP tools that agents can use for browser automation (PRD US-8).
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/browser/browser-tools.ts with tool definitions

Tools: navigate(url), click(selector), fill(selector, value), screenshot(), assertText(selector, expected), assertElement(selector), getConsoleErrors(), evaluateScript(code), waitForSelector(selector, timeout)
Each tool uses the BrowserEngine abstraction
Create a 'browser-test' Ability record that wraps these tools
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create browser REST API endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need API endpoints to control the dev browser.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/routes/browser.ts as a Fastify plugin
POST /api/browser/launch launches a browser instance
POST /api/browser/navigate accepts { url: string } and navigates
POST /api/browser/screenshot returns a base64 screenshot
POST /api/browser/evaluate accepts { script: string } and returns result
POST /api/browser/close shuts down the browser
GET /api/browser/status returns whether browser is running
Input validation on all POST bodies
Register routes in server/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create dev server proxy endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to proxy the running dev server through the backend for the browser preview.
acceptanceCriteria: Add GET /api/browser/proxy/* route that proxies requests to the project's running dev server
Reads the dev server port from the localhost runner status
Proxies HTML, CSS, JS, and asset requests
Adds CORS headers for the Insomniac frontend origin
Returns 502 if dev server is not running
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create browser API hooks for frontend
userStories.description: As a developer, I need TanStack Query hooks for browser control from the frontend.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/api/browser.ts with useBrowserStatus(), useLaunchBrowser(), useNavigate(), useScreenshot(), useCloseBrowser() hooks
Screenshot hook returns base64 image data
All mutations invalidate 'browser-status' query key
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create Dev Browser view in bottom panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a live preview of my dev server in the Browser tab.
acceptanceCriteria: Update BottomPanel Browser tab with real browser integration
Shows iframe pointing to the proxy endpoint when dev server is running
Launch button starts the browser if not running
URL bar shows current URL and allows navigation
Refresh button reloads the iframe
Shows 'Dev server not running' message with start button when no server is active
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add screenshot capture to Dev Browser
userStories.description: As a user, I want to capture screenshots of the current browser viewport.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Screenshot button to the Browser tab toolbar
Clicking captures a screenshot via the API
Shows the screenshot in a preview overlay
Copy to clipboard button copies the base64 image
Save button downloads as PNG file
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add console panel to Dev Browser
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see browser console output in real time.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a Console sub-panel below the browser preview in the Browser tab
WebSocket events ('browser:console') stream console.log/warn/error from the browser
Each entry shows: level icon (info/warn/error), timestamp, message text
Color-coded by level: info=default, warn=amber, error=red
Clear button to reset the console log
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add agent activity feed to Dev Browser
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see what the agent is doing in the browser in real time.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an Agent Activity section to the Browser tab
WebSocket events ('browser:agent-action') show agent actions: 'clicking #submit-btn', 'asserting text matches Welcome'
Each entry shows: timestamp, action description, status (pending/done/error)
Scrolls to latest entry automatically
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add 'Inspect in agent' context menu action
userStories.description: As a user, I want to right-click an element in the Dev Browser and send it to the agent for fixing (PRD US-25).
acceptanceCriteria: Add POST /api/browser/inspect-in-agent endpoint accepting { selector: string, description: string, projectId: string }
Endpoint creates a single pipeline stage with the prompt: 'Fix this element: [selector] — [description]'
Returns the created stage ID
Frontend: add a button/action in the Browser tab that opens a dialog for selector and description input
Submit sends the request and shows confirmation
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Full visual editor overlay with click-to-select is deferred to a polish pass. This story implements the API and a simple form-based interface.
